summary: The src/lib/concerts.ts library is a mission-critical module with 25+ exported functions for concert CRUD, pagination, and statistics. Assessment identifies resilience gaps (missing error handling, unprotected Prisma calls), performance risks (N+1 queries in some code paths, synchronous band transformations, raw SQL queries without timeouts), and opportunities for optimization (connection pooling configuration, caching strategies, transaction isolation).

failure_modes: Connection pool exhaustion (too many concurrent queries)
Constraint violations (unique violations on UserConcert, ConcertBand)
Raw SQL query timeout (complex JSON aggregations)
dependency_map.current_handling: No retry logic, no timeouts on Prisma operations, no circuit breaker, connection pool defaults used (may be insufficient for concurrent load)
dependency_map.recommended_handling: Implement: (1) exponential backoff retry on transient Prisma errors; (2) explicit timeout on all Prisma queries; (3) circuit breaker for repeated failures; (4) tune connection pool based on concurrency profile; (5) handle transaction deadlock with retry; (6) add context-aware logging for all DB errors

failure_modes: Network timeout fetching concert data in API routes
dependency_map.current_handling: No timeout on request.json(); auth calls use implicit timeouts from Next.js; generic catch-all in POST
dependency_map.recommended_handling: Add explicit timeout when parsing request body; add specific error handling for Sentry vs auth failures

dependency_map.current_handling: Called in createNewConcertWithUser and updateConcert; no retry, no timeout, no rate limit awareness
dependency_map.recommended_handling: Wrap getGeocodingData in retry with exponential backoff; add timeout (5s connect, 10s read); fall back gracefully if geocoding fails

failure_modes: Raw SQL query timeout (computeUserConcertStatistics band aggregation with JSON parsing)
SQL syntax errors (parameter injection risk, though Prisma.sql guards against it)
Fallback catch block hides migration status (supportingActIds column)
dependency_map.current_handling: Try-catch fallback in computeUserConcertStatistics; no timeout; exception swallowed by fallback
dependency_map.recommended_handling: Add explicit query timeout via Postgres statement_timeout; expose migration errors distinctly; log fallback activations

error_handling_gaps.gap: getGeocodingData() call has no error handling; if Photon API fails, entire concert creation fails with 500
error_handling_gaps.impact: User sees generic 'Failed to create concert' instead of 'Geocoding service unavailable, concert created without city data'
error_handling_gaps.fix: Wrap getGeocodingData in try-catch; if it fails, set normalizedCity to null and log warning; return concert anyway. Distinguish transient (retry-able) vs permanent (fail-fast) errors.

error_handling_gaps.gap: Transaction with create/delete inside; no handling for transaction failure (deadlock, constraint violation); if match-merge logic fails mid-transaction, state is inconsistent
error_handling_gaps.impact: Concurrent edits on same concert from multiple users could cause duplicate attendees or orphaned records; requires manual DB recovery
error_handling_gaps.fix: Add try-catch around entire forkConcertForUser flow; on transaction failure, log with context (concertId, userId) and re-throw as distinct error; implement retry on deadlock with exponential backoff

error_handling_gaps.gap: Catch block catches all errors and returns generic 500; ConcertAlreadyExistsError handled explicitly, but other DB errors (constraint violations, auth errors, Sentry capture errors) not differentiated
error_handling_gaps.impact: Clients can't distinguish user error (concert already exists) from system error (database constraint failure) from infrastructure error (Sentry down)
error_handling_gaps.fix: Expand catch block: (1) catch Prisma UniqueConstraintError and map to 409 Conflict with specific message; (2) catch Prisma.PrismaClientValidationError and return 400 Bad Request; (3) distinguish transient vs permanent errors; (4) only capture to Sentry if not user-caused error

error_handling_gaps.gap: Band lookup via prisma.band.findUnique returns null silently if band slug invalid; subsequent raw SQL query returns empty result; no error signal to caller
error_handling_gaps.impact: Invalid band slug treated same as band with no concerts; client can't distinguish 'band not found' from 'user attended 0 concerts by this band'
error_handling_gaps.fix: After band lookup, throw distinct error if band not found: 'throw new Error(`Band not found: ${slug}`)'; or return distinct response object with notFound flag

error_handling_gaps.location: computeUserConcertStatistics() raw SQL query with fallback
error_handling_gaps.gap: If supportingActIds column missing (migration not run), fallback catch block silently activates; caller doesn't know if stats are complete or degraded
error_handling_gaps.impact: In production, if migration partially applied, stats appear correct but exclude user's supporting act selection data
error_handling_gaps.fix: Log migration status distinctly (warn if fallback activates); include degraded-mode flag in response; return stats with 'degradedMode: true' so client can show warning

error_handling_gaps.location: All Prisma queries (findMany, create, update, delete, aggregate)
error_handling_gaps.gap: No explicit timeout configured; long-running query can block request indefinitely if database is slow or overloaded
error_handling_gaps.impact: Single slow query can cause cascading timeouts in Next.js edge runtime (12s limit), leading to dropped requests and user experience degradation
error_handling_gaps.fix: Add context.timeout() around all prisma operations; wrap in timeout promise that rejects after 10s (for read), 15s (for write); differentiate timeout errors from other DB errors

retry_recommendations.strategy: Exponential backoff with jitter; only retry on transient HTTP errors (5xx, timeout, connection reset)
retry_recommendations.on_exhaustion: Log error with coordinates, allow concert creation with normalizedCity = null, return 201 with warning field

retry_recommendations.strategy: Retry entire fork operation on deadlock; exponential backoff between retries
retry_recommendations.on_exhaustion: Log conflict with both concert IDs and user ID; return 409 Conflict with message 'Another user is editing this concert, please refresh'

retry_recommendations.strategy: Automatic fallback to simpler query; log that fallback was used for monitoring
retry_recommendations.on_exhaustion: Fallback query activated; returns degraded stats (headliner only, no supporting acts) with degradedMode flag

idempotency_assessment.mechanism: Duplicate detection via findMatchingConcert (date + location + headliner); raises ConcertAlreadyExistsError on re-invocation with same data
idempotency_assessment.coverage: Only covers concerts with explicit headliner; headliner-less concerts are NOT checked for duplicates

idempotency_assessment.mechanism: UpdateConcertInput is fully specified; multiple invocations with same input produce same output
idempotency_assessment.risk: Low - band edits may trigger fork, but fork is deterministic given input
idempotency_assessment.caveat: Fork logic depends on current concert state; if another user modifies concert between retries, result may differ

idempotency_assessment.mechanism: Second delete returns 404 (concert already deleted); caller must handle 404 as success
idempotency_assessment.risk: Client must treat 404 as idempotent success; not all clients do this
idempotency_assessment.fix: Return 200 { success: true } on second delete (already gone) instead of 404; or document that 404 means 'already deleted, operation succeeded'

performance_concerns.concern: N+1 queries in transformConcertSync when prefetchedBands Map is incomplete
performance_concerns.location: transformConcertSync() lines 172-174 filters supportingActBands; if band missing from prefetchedBands, returns undefined
performance_concerns.impact: If prefetch misses a band ID, transform silently drops it from output; no warning; UI shows incomplete band list
performance_concerns.current_code: supportingActBands.map((o) => prefetchedBands.get(o.bandId)).filter((b): b is PrismaBand => b != null)
performance_concerns.fix: Log warning if any band ID not in map; optionally fetch missing bands inline (fallback), but this reintroduces N+1 queries

performance_concerns.concern: computeUserConcertStatistics performs sequential Promise.all but with nested queries inside (lines 1771-1854)
performance_concerns.impact: Band stats computation: (1) raw $queryRaw for top 10 band IDs; (2) await prisma.band.findMany; (3) then sequential Promise.all over bandIds to count concerts per band - this is N+1-like pattern in the aggregate flow
performance_concerns.fix: Consolidate band counting into single raw SQL query: SELECT band_id, COUNT(*) FROM effective_bands GROUP BY band_id ORDER BY COUNT DESC LIMIT 5; avoid sequential Promise.all

performance_concerns.concern: getConcertsPaginated calls transformConcertsBatch which batches band fetches, but raw SQL queries (getConcertsPaginatedForUserByBand) perform N+1 for counts
performance_concerns.location: getConcertsPaginatedForUserByBand() lines 1281-1405 plus subsequent getConcertsPaginatedForUser calls
performance_concerns.impact: For band-filtered pagination with 20 items: (1) raw SQL to get IDs; (2) findMany to fetch concerts; (3) SEPARATE nested band lookups to fill prefetchedBands map - adds latency
performance_concerns.current_pattern: Raw SQL fetch IDs → findMany concerts → transformConcertsBatch (which fetches supporting act bands)
performance_concerns.fix: Include band data in initial raw SQL query via JOIN or ensure band IDs from supporting acts are collected before any I/O; use better SQL query design

performance_concerns.concern: Connection pool may be undersized for concurrent load at scale
performance_concerns.impact: If 100+ concurrent requests arrive, default pool size (10) leads to queue starvation; requests block waiting for connection; timeouts accumulate
performance_concerns.fix: Configure pool explicitly: new Pool({ ..., max: 20, idleTimeoutMillis: 30000, connectionTimeoutMillis: 5000 }); or set statement_timeout in connection string

performance_concerns.concern: getAllConcerts() loads ALL concerts into memory for transform; scales poorly as DB grows
performance_concerns.impact: Production: if database has 100k concerts, query + transform loads all 100k into memory in single pass; OOM risk if many concurrent requests
performance_concerns.fix: This function appears unused in current codebase (grep to verify); if needed, add pagination limit or streaming cursor; or mark as deprecated with pagination alternative

performance_concerns.concern: getAllYears() and getAllCities() load all distinct values into memory then sort
performance_concerns.impact: Low risk (bounded by unique years ~100, unique cities ~1k) but inefficient; sorting in application instead of SQL
performance_concerns.fix: Add ORDER BY in SQL query: Prisma doesn't expose direct SQL order for groupBy; use raw SQL or accept current approach for now

caching_recommendations.what_to_cache: User's band statistics (mostSeenBands, top 5 unique bands)
caching_recommendations.pattern: Memoize or Redis with TTL; re-compute on concert create/update/delete
caching_recommendations.current_pattern: No caching; computed fresh on every request to /api/admin/stats or dashboard
caching_recommendations.rationale: Band stats are expensive (complex SQL join with JSON parsing); rarely change except when user adds/edits concerts; 1-hour TTL balances freshness vs latency

caching_recommendations.what_to_cache: Concert pagination cursors and cursor->ID mapping
caching_recommendations.pattern: In-memory cache (Map or Redis) with 5-min TTL; map cursor string to concert ID and date for keyset pagination
caching_recommendations.rationale: Cursor-based pagination requires looking up concert date by cursor ID; if cursor is stale, lookup fails; caching cursor->ID mapping speeds up subsequent pages

caching_recommendations.what_to_cache: Band metadata (name, slug, image, genres) after first fetch
caching_recommendations.pattern: DataLoader-style batch cache within single request; persistent Redis cache across requests with 24h TTL
caching_recommendations.rationale: Band data is mostly static; fetched repeatedly in transformConcertsBatch; caching reduces DB round-trips for prefetch

caching_recommendations.what_to_cache: Concert matching results (findMatchingConcert coordinate tolerance searches)
caching_recommendations.pattern: Short-lived cache (30s) keyed by (date, lat, lon, headlinerId); invalidate on concert create/update
caching_recommendations.rationale: Within same request batch, multiple create/update ops may search same coordinates; cache prevents repeated DB queries

timeout_recommendations.service: Prisma database operations (findMany, create, update, delete)
timeout_recommendations.rationale: Next.js edge runtime enforces 12s limit for serverless functions; database operations must complete within this; 10s for read ensures room for transformation; 15s for write accounts for transaction overhead

timeout_recommendations.service: Prisma $queryRaw for complex JSON band aggregations
timeout_recommendations.rationale: Complex raw SQL queries (computeUserConcertStatistics band stats) can scan large datasets; 5s timeout prevents runaway queries; fallback to simpler query if timeout
External HTTP API; fast response expected; 5s read timeout prevents blocking concert creation indefinitely

timeout_recommendations.service: request.json() body parsing in API routes
timeout_recommendations.rationale: Prevent hung requests from clients sending incomplete bodies; 5s is reasonable for typical payloads; fail-fast to free server resources

risks.description: Geocoding failure during concert creation leaves DB in inconsistent state if transaction not used
risks.impact: Concert created without city data; user sees concert without location tag; admin reports show incomplete data
risks.mitigation: Wrap createNewConcertWithUser in try-catch; if geocoding fails, still create concert with normalizedCity=null; return 201 with warning; make geocoding optional, not blocking

risks.description: Fork transaction deadlock under concurrent multi-user edits causes cascading request failures
risks.impact: Edit requests return 500 instead of 409; UI shows generic error; user loses data if client-side cache not preserved
risks.mitigation: Implement retry with exponential backoff for Prisma deadlock errors; return 409 with user-friendly message on 3 retries exhausted; log deadlock for monitoring

risks.description: Missing indexes on pagination queries (date, normalizedCity, userId+concertId) cause full table scans
risks.impact: As concert count grows to 100k+, pagination queries slow from ~50ms to ~500ms+; user experience degrades; costs increase
risks.mitigation: Verify Prisma schema has @@index on date, normalizedCity, userId, concertId; add composite index on (userId, concertId) for UserConcert; monitor slow query log

risks.description: Connection pool exhaustion if concurrent requests spike (e.g., when user shares public profile link)
risks.impact: New requests queue indefinitely; timeouts accumulate; cascading failures; partial outage
risks.mitigation: Configure pg.Pool with explicit max: 20, idleTimeoutMillis: 30000; add circuit breaker that returns 503 if connection queue exceeds threshold; monitor pool utilization

risks.description: Raw SQL queries vulnerable to logic errors; migration of supportingActIds column not detected
risks.impact: If migration not run, raw SQL queries fail; fallback silently activates; stats are incomplete; data loss goes unnoticed
risks.mitigation: Add distinct migration check at startup; fail fast if required columns missing; expose degraded-mode flag in responses; log migration status changes

risks.description: Sentry capture in error handler can block request if Sentry slow or down
risks.impact: API response times spike; cascading timeouts; Sentry errors not useful if system already under stress
risks.mitigation: Capture Sentry async (no await); implement timeout on Sentry capture (2s max); fall back to local logging if Sentry unreachable

must_implement: Add timeout() wrapper on all Prisma queries to prevent indefinite blocking
Implement error handling for getGeocodingData failures; allow concert creation without geocoding
Add specific Prisma error handling in API routes (constraint violations -> 409, validation -> 400)
Implement retry with backoff for Prisma deadlock errors in forkConcertForUser
Configure pg.Pool connection limits explicitly (max: 20, idleTimeoutMillis: 30000)
should_implement: Distinguish transient vs permanent errors; expose error type to client (retryable vs not)
Add band statistics caching with 1-hour TTL; invalidate on concert mutations
Optimize computeUserConcertStatistics raw SQL to avoid N+1-like pattern in band counting
Log migration status; expose degradedMode flag if supportingActIds column not present
Add circuit breaker for repeated geocoding failures; skip geocoding after 3 consecutive failures
consider_implementing: Verify indexes on Concert(date, normalizedCity, userId) and UserConcert(userId, concertId)
Implement DELETE idempotency: return 200 instead of 404 on second delete
Add cursor->ID mapping cache for pagination (30s TTL)
Deprecate getAllConcerts() if unused; replace with paginated version
Profile concert transform memory usage; consider streaming responses for large result sets
